description: REQ-020 EARS clause states: 'The main feed area SHALL be empty' during Loading state (behavioral-spec.md line 485). CRIT-018 passThreshold reinforces this: 'In Loading state the main area shows only a centered loading affordance alongside the header shell. In UnexpectedError state no main content renders.' The implementation in AppShell.svelte:72-92 renders `<main>` containing two skeleton-cards inside a status region during Loading. The skeleton-cards are not 'empty main feed area' — they are skeleton-card elements with rendered DOM, height 32px, margin 8px. This contradicts both the spec EARS literal and the CRIT-018 wording. The 'centered loading affordance' belongs in the header-adjacent shell area per REQ-020 description (color: Pure White background, spinner with aria-hidden=true), not inside <main>. Note: this is distinct from FIND-601 — FIND-601 is the missing header; FIND-602 is the wrong content of <main>.

evidence.snippet:
{#if state === "Loading"}
{/if}
